Use Case · Time Tracking

Best Construction Software for Time Tracking (2026)

Knowing your actual labor cost per job starts with accurate time tracking. Here's what works in the field.

Labor is typically 30–50% of a construction project's cost. If you don't know your actual labor hours per job, you can't know your real margin — and you can't improve your estimating. Effective time tracking in construction needs to work for people on job sites, not just in offices.

What good construction time tracking requires

  • Mobile clock-in/out: Workers must be able to log time from their phone on the job site
  • GPS verification: Confirm workers are actually at the job when they clock in
  • Job and cost code assignment: Time must be attributed to specific projects and categories (framing, electrical rough-in, etc.) for job costing to work
  • Supervisor approval: Time cards should route to a supervisor for review before payroll export
  • Payroll integration: Approved hours should flow to QuickBooks Payroll, Gusto, or similar — not require manual re-entry
  • Overtime tracking: Automatic tracking of overtime thresholds for payroll compliance

Top platforms for construction time tracking

Buildertrend — Best integrated time tracking for residential GCs

Buildertrend's time clock module lets field workers clock in/out with GPS verification through the mobile app. Hours are assigned to specific projects and tasks, which feeds directly into job costing. Supervisors review and approve time cards, and approved hours can export to payroll platforms.

The key advantage: time tracked in Buildertrend feeds the same job costing system as your estimates and purchase orders. You can see labor cost vs. budget in real time, not weeks later after payroll closes.

Buildertrend's time tracking feeds real-time job costing — see your labor cost vs. budget on every project.

Try Buildertrend

Houzz Pro — Basic time tracking for smaller crews

Houzz Pro includes time tracking functionality at its accessible price point. For smaller contractors with straightforward needs — logging hours, linking to projects, and generating basic reports — it handles the basics well.

Houzz Pro time tracking is simple and effective for smaller residential contractors.

Try Houzz Pro Free

JobTread — Best job-cost-linked time tracking

JobTread's time tracking is tightly integrated with its job costing system. Labor hours logged through the mobile app become actual labor costs on the job in real time. The system supports cost codes, which means you can track time by trade phase and see exactly where your labor is going on each project.

JobTread's time tracking updates job costing in real time — know your labor margin on every job.

Try JobTread

Raken — Best for daily report + time card combo

Raken combines daily reports with time tracking in a mobile-first format that field crews actually complete. Workers log time while completing their daily report — two tasks in one workflow. For GCs who need both consistent daily documentation and accurate labor hours, Raken's approach is efficient.

Dedicated time tracking tools

For contractors who need time tracking but don't need a full construction management platform, dedicated tools like Clockify, TSheets (now QuickBooks Time), or Busybusy offer GPS-verified mobile time tracking with payroll integration at lower price points. These can be effective supplements if your primary construction software lacks strong time tracking.

The job costing connection

Time tracking is only valuable if the hours get attributed correctly to specific jobs and cost categories. A platform where workers clock in "on a job" but all hours pool into one bucket doesn't give you job costing data — it just gives you total hours. Make sure your time tracking solution supports cost code assignment so field labor becomes actionable job cost data.
